machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: EscC = SctC secretin of the LEE injectisome; review also states 'EscC belongs to the secretin family of proteins that function as channels for secretion ... across the OM'. A/E pathogen (EPEC/EHEC) T3SS, same LEE apparatus present in O157:H7 Sakai.

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: EscD = SctD-family IM ring; 'EscD is believed to form the outermost IM ring that surrounds the EscJ ring'.

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins EscD and EscJ form two concentric rings in the inner membrane (IM) and the EscC protein forms the outer membrane (OM) ring
machinery_genes.note: EscJ = SctJ-family IM-ring lipoprotein; 'anchored to the outer leaflet of the IM through an N-terminal lipid modification site'.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscR = SctR export-gate component of the membrane export apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscS = SctS export-gate component.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscT = SctT export-gate component.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens, is built of five different proteins: EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, and EscV (EscRSTUV)
machinery_genes.note: EscU = SctU; YscU/FlhB family autoprotease switch; 'EscU interacts directly with EscP and EscI, and both interactions have also been implicated in the switching event'.

machinery_genes.quote: EscV, the major constituent of the T3 export apparatus, is also composed of two main domains: an N-terminal region with eight transmembrane helices and a C-terminal cytoplasmic domain
machinery_genes.note: EscV = SctV (FlhA/YscV family) major export-apparatus ring.

machinery_genes.quote: The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins (Figure), all of which are important for T3SS function
machinery_genes.note: EscN = SctN ATPase; 'The crystal structure of the ATPase EscN has been solved, and like FliI, it shows structural similarity to the F1-ATPase'.

machinery_genes.quote: The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins (Figure), all of which are important for T3SS function
machinery_genes.note: EscO (formerly EscA) = SctO; 'EscO is the evolutionary counterpart of the flagellar FliJ protein', stimulates EscN ATPase activity.

machinery_genes.role: ATPase-complex stator / C-ring link (negative regulator of ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: The ATPase complex is formed by the EscN, EscL, and EscO proteins (Figure), all of which are important for T3SS function
machinery_genes.note: EscL = SctL (FliH-like); 'EscN interacts with EscL, a negative regulator that inhibits its ATPase activity'; also implicated as a sorting-platform component.

machinery_genes.quote: the C-ring is proposed to be a cytoplasmic annular structure located at the base of the basal body, formed mainly by the EscQ protein (formerly known as SepQ), which belongs to the YscQ/FliN (SctQ) protein family
machinery_genes.note: EscQ (formerly SepQ) = SctQ C-ring/sorting-platform protein.

machinery_genes.quote: in A/E pathogens the sorting platform might be formed by EscQ, EscL, and EscK (formerly Orf4), a component that has been associated with the SctK protein family
machinery_genes.note: EscK (formerly Orf4) = SctK; 'Like other members of the SctK protein family such as OrgA ... EscK is critical for T3 secretion'.

machinery_genes.quote: It is presumed that the OM and IM rings are connected through a periplasmic inner rod formed by the oligomerization of the EscI protein
machinery_genes.note: EscI = SctI inner-rod subunit; interacts with EscU and EscP and the EtgA lytic enzyme.

machinery_genes.quote: The needle is a superhelical hollow structure comprised of multiple copies of the EscF protein (Wilson et al.,), which is essential for the secretion of all T3 substrates
machinery_genes.note: EscF = SctF needle subunit; associates with EscC, EscD, EscJ and the EspA filament.

machinery_genes.quote: EscP, which directly interacts with EscF, measures needle length upon intermittent secretion during needle assembly
machinery_genes.note: EscP = SctP (T3S4 family) molecular ruler; switches substrate specificity via EscU.

machinery_genes.quote: In A/E pathogens, elimination of SepL abolishes secretion of the translocators and increases secretion of effectors
machinery_genes.note: SepL = gatekeeper (SctW functional class; YopN/InvE/MxiC/SsaL family). Part of the SepL/SepD switch that controls translocator-vs-effector secretion; 'SepL also interacts with the export gate component EscV'.

machinery_genes.quote: In EPEC and EHEC SepL interacts with SepD, whose deletion in A/E pathogens results in the same phenotype as the null mutant
machinery_genes.note: SepD = SepL partner in the gatekeeper switch (SctW functional class). LEE-encoded apparatus regulator of substrate secretion order, not a transcriptional regulator and not an effector.

machinery_genes.quote: it is assembled by the polymerization of multiple subunits of the EspA protein ... forming a hollow structure of 12 nm in width that allows the passage of substrates
machinery_genes.note: EspA filament is part of the secretion apparatus (A/E-pathogen-specific needle extension / hydrophilic translocator), not an injected effector. Per the apparatus brief, translocon proteins count as machinery.

machinery_genes.quote: The translocation pore is a protein complex formed by the hetero-oligomerization of EspB and EspD subunits
machinery_genes.note: EspB = host-membrane translocon component (apparatus per brief). It is additionally translocated into host cells, but its pore-forming role makes it machinery.

machinery_genes.quote: The translocation pore is a protein complex formed by the hetero-oligomerization of EspB and EspD subunits
machinery_genes.note: EspD = host-membrane translocon component (apparatus per brief); 'EspD was the only bacterial protein membrane-associated' during pore formation.

machinery_genes.role: peptidoglycan-lytic enzyme that enables injectisome assembly across the PG layer
machinery_genes.quote: In EPEC this task is accomplished by the use of a specialized PG degrading enzyme named EtgA (Pallen et al.,) that localizes to the periplasm to exert its PG lytic activity and which is required for efficient T3SS assembly
machinery_genes.note: EtgA is the LEE-encoded specialized lytic transglycosylase required for T3SS assembly (interacts with and is stimulated by the inner rod EscI). Not in the brief's Sct table because it is an accessory assembly factor rather than a structural ring; included as apparatus because it is a dedicated injectisome-assembly component, not an effector/chaperone/regulator. Lower-confidence inclusion on the structural-core question.

notes: The instance label prophage_CP-933U with effectors ECs2714/ECs2715 refers to NON-LEE effectors carried on a Sakai prophage; these are translocated substrates, not machinery. In E. coli O157:H7 Sakai there is a single T3SS injectisome, encoded by the chromosomal Locus of Enterocyte Effacement (LEE, 41 conserved genes in operons LEE1-LEE5), which secretes both LEE and non-LEE (prophage-borne) effectors. The machinery genes listed are the LEE-encoded esc/sep/esp apparatus. Apparatus components and naming quotes are from Gaytan et al 2016 (Front Cell Infect Microbiol), an open-access review of the A/E-pathogen (EPEC/EHEC) T3SS that names every component verbatim; the same LEE injectisome is present in O157:H7 strains including EDL933 and Sakai. The two complete-LEE-sequence papers are cited as apparatus sources: Perna et al 1998 (EDL933, O157:H7, same serotype as Sakai) and Elliott et al 1998 (EPEC E2348/69, which established the esc/sep nomenclature). Coverage: OM secretin (EscC), IM rings (EscD, EscJ), export apparatus (EscR, EscS, EscT, EscU, EscV), ATPase complex (EscN, EscO, EscL), C-ring/sorting platform (EscQ, EscK), inner rod (EscI), needle (EscF), ruler (EscP), gatekeeper switch (SepL, SepD), translocon (EspA filament + EspB/EspD pore), and the assembly lytic transglycosylase EtgA. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted/translocated effectors (Tir, Map, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ and the non-LEE effectors ECs2714/ECs2715), the adhesin intimin (Eae) and its receptor Tir, the type III chaperones (CesT, CesD, CesD2, CesAB, CesF, CesL, EscE, EscG), and the transcriptional regulators (Ler, GrlA, GrlR). Per the apparatus brief, the translocon proteins EspA/EspB/EspD are counted as machinery (host-membrane pore + filament) despite EspB also being translocated. Lowest-confidence call is EtgA (an accessory assembly factor, not a structural ring); SepD is named only via its SepL partnership. Flagellar export homologs (Flh/Fli) are explicitly not counted.
